title: Spark Entity Type File
description: Defines an entity type (persistent object) and its associated queries for a MintPlayer Spark application. Place these files in App_Data/Model/.

$schema.description: Optional JSON Schema reference for editor IntelliSense.

queries.description: Queries associated with this entity type. Each query defines a data source and how results are fetched and displayed.

TranslatedString.description: A translated string where keys are ISO language codes (e.g., 'en', 'fr', 'nl') and values are the translated text.

EntityTypeDefinition.description: Defines the metadata, attributes, layout, and behavior of a persistent object (entity type) in the Spark framework.

id.description: Unique identifier (GUID) for this entity type definition.

name.description: The programmatic name of the entity type (e.g., 'Car', 'Person'). Used internally for resolution and routing.

description.description: Human-readable translated description of this entity type.

clrType.description: The full CLR type name of the entity class (e.g., 'Fleet.Entities.Car'). Must match the C# class used in RavenDB.

alias.description: Optional URL-friendly alias for this entity type. Used as an alternative to the GUID in URLs (e.g., /po/car instead of /po/{guid}). If not set, auto-generated from Name by lowercasing.

queryType.description: The CLR type name of the projection type used for RavenDB index queries. Set when a projection class has [FromIndex] attribute linking to an index for this entity (e.g., 'Fleet.Indexes.VCar').

indexName.description: The name of the RavenDB index to use for list queries when queryType is set. Derived from the IndexRegistry based on the index class name (e.g., 'Cars_Overview').

displayFormat.description: Template string with {PropertyName} placeholders for building a formatted display value (e.g., '{Street}, {PostalCode} {City}').

displayAttribute.description: Fallback: single attribute name to use as display value when displayFormat is not specified (e.g., 'LicensePlate').

tabs.description: Tab definitions for organizing attribute groups on the detail/edit page.

groups.description: Attribute group definitions for visually grouping related attributes within a tab.

attributes.description: The attribute (field/property) definitions for this entity type.

queries.description: Query aliases or IDs to display as related query tables on the detail page. Each entry references a SparkQuery that accepts parent context.

AttributeTab.description: Defines a tab on the entity detail/edit page for organizing attribute groups.

label.description: Translated display label for the tab.

order.description: Sort order of the tab. Lower numbers appear first.

columnCount.description: Number of columns for the grid layout within this tab.

AttributeGroup.description: Defines a visual group for related attributes within a tab.

label.description: Translated display label for the group.

tab.description: References an AttributeTab.id to assign this group to a tab. When null, the group is placed on the first/default tab.

order.description: Sort order of the group within its tab. Lower numbers appear first.

EntityAttributeDefinition.description: Defines a single attribute (field/property) on an entity type, including its data type, visibility, validation, and rendering options.

name.description: The property name on the CLR entity class (e.g., 'LicensePlate', 'FirstName').

label.description: Translated display label shown in the UI for this attribute.

dataType.description: The data type of the attribute. Built-in types: 'string', 'number', 'date', 'boolean', 'color', 'Reference', 'AsDetail'. Custom types may also be used.

isRequired.description: When true, the attribute must have a value. The framework generates a 'required' validation rule automatically.

isVisible.description: When false, the attribute is hidden from all UI views.

isReadOnly.description: When true, the attribute is displayed but cannot be edited.

order.description: Sort order of the attribute within its group or tab. Lower numbers appear first.

query.description: For Reference attributes, the query name or alias used to fetch the list of selectable items (e.g., 'GetCompanies').

referenceType.description: For Reference attributes, specifies the target entity type's CLR type name (e.g., 'HR.Entities.Company').

asDetailType.description: For AsDetail attributes, specifies the nested entity type's CLR type name (e.g., 'HR.Entities.Address').

isArray.description: When true, the attribute represents an array/collection of AsDetail objects (e.g., CarreerJob[]). When false, a single AsDetail object.

editMode.description: For array AsDetail attributes, controls how items are edited. 'modal' (default) opens a dialog; 'inline' edits directly in the table row.

lookupReferenceType.description: For LookupReference attributes, specifies the lookup reference type name (e.g., 'CarStatus', 'CarBrand').

inCollectionType.description: When false, this attribute exists only in the projection type (computed by index). Not present in the collection entity. Used for list views only.

inQueryType.description: When false, this attribute exists only in the collection type (not projected by the index). Used for detail/edit views only.

showedOn.description: Controls on which pages the attribute is displayed. 'Query' = list views, 'PersistentObject' = detail/edit views, 'Query, PersistentObject' = both.

rules.description: Validation rules applied to this attribute.

group.description: References an AttributeGroup.id to assign this attribute to a group. When null, the attribute is placed in a default (ungrouped) section.

columnSpan.description: Number of grid columns this attribute spans within a tab's column layout. Defaults to 1 when not specified.

renderer.description: Optional renderer name that tells the frontend which custom component to use for read-only display (detail page and query list). Examples: 'video-player', 'color-swatch', 'markdown'.

rendererOptions.description: Optional configuration for the renderer, passed as-is to the frontend component. Example: { "width": 480, "height": 270, "autoplay": false }.

ValidationRule.description: A validation rule applied to an entity attribute. The 'type' field determines which other properties are relevant.

type.description: The validation rule type. Built-in types: 'required', 'minLength', 'maxLength', 'range', 'regex', 'email', 'url'.

value.description: The rule parameter. For 'minLength'/'maxLength': a number. For 'regex': a regular expression string.

message.description: Custom translated validation error message. When not set, a default message is generated using translation keys.

SparkQuery.description: Defines a data query that fetches a list of entities, optionally with sorting, pagination, and streaming.

description.description: Translated human-readable description of the query, shown in the UI.

source.description: Query data source. Two formats supported: 'Database.PropertyName' resolves to an IRavenQueryable property on SparkContext; 'Custom.MethodName' resolves to a method on the entity's Actions class.

alias.description: Optional URL-friendly alias for this query. Used as an alternative to the GUID in URLs (e.g., /query/cars instead of /query/{guid}). If not set, auto-generated from Name by stripping 'Get' prefix and lowercasing.

sortColumns.description: Multi-column sort specification. Each entry specifies a property name and direction. Applied in order: first entry = primary sort, subsequent = tiebreakers.

renderMode.description: Controls how query results are rendered in the UI.

indexName.description: Optional RavenDB index name. When specified, the query will use this index and return the projection type registered in the IndexRegistry.

useProjection.description: When true, indicates this query uses a projection type (from IndexRegistry) rather than the full entity type.

entityType.description: The entity/view-model type name this query returns (e.g., 'Person', 'CompanyProductsOverview'). When set, the framework uses the corresponding EntityTypeDefinition to map results. When not set, the type is inferred from the source.

isStreamingQuery.description: When true, this query supports WebSocket streaming. The frontend opens a WebSocket to /spark/queries/{id}/stream and receives snapshot + patch messages.

SortColumn.description: Specifies a sort column with property name and direction.

property.description: The property name to sort by.

direction.description: Sort direction: 'asc' for ascending, 'desc' for descending.
